threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep stepping off the Overground at the wrong exits, still think 'the Gospel' means a Sunday service, not a local bar.
Last week on the 59, a guy started singing opera. Everyone was silent, then joined in. Brixton magic at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a neighbor's cat meowing along to a street musician's guitar on the corner of Weserstraße. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a kebab shop in Kreuzberg that's open 24/7 and serves a special vegan döner with curry sauce. Only in Berlin will you taste that combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their nonchalant reaction to the sight of a guy juggling fire while waiting for the 14. Tourists stare, we just keep scrolling.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched a guy in a tuxedo serenade the bus driver with a ukulele as we waited at Market. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. They miss the real heart in the smaller plazas, where locals gather and actually live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their struggle to pronounce 'Gràcia' correctly—it's not 'Gracia' like in Spanish, but with the Catalan twist. It's endearing.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look for a seat on the bus instead of grabbing a pole like it’s a dance floor. You'll learn soon enough.
Last week on the 322, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic soul tunes. Didn’t know we had a Brixton Idol in our midst!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football in a backyard while a couple hosted a wine tasting next door. Berlin life in full swing.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can still find random parties in abandoned buildings here. Last weekend, an old factory turned club had a silent disco that felt like stepping back in time.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can navigate the Muni schedule chaos and still find time for tacos at La Taqueria, even during the delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni ride where a guy was singing opera while dodging the dog on the tracks. Classic San Francisco—both majestic and chaotic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all bars close at 1 AM, but the real spots stay open until 3 or 4, filled with locals discussing life over late-night vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still walk into bars asking for a 'café con leche' at 8 PM, totally oblivious to the evening vermut culture.
